description_long: This shotgun features a robust, utilitarian design with a predominantly black metal receiver and barrel, complemented by brown wooden elements on the stock, fore-end, and grip. The barrel is cylindrical and extends forward, with a squared-off muzzle and no visible ribbing or venting. The stock is straight and solid, providing a stable shoulder rest, while the grip is integrated into the wooden lower section. The trigger and trigger guard are clearly visible, with the guard being rounded and of moderate size. The magazine is curved and protrudes downward, suggesting a box magazine configuration, and the overall form is compact and functional, suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, black and brown shotgun with a solid wooden stock, curved magazine, and a simple, utilitarian design. The metal receiver and barrel contrast with the wooden grip and stock.
description_view_0: The shotgun is seen from the front, showing the barrel's end, the top of the receiver, and the alignment of the wooden stock and fore-end.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the full length of the shotgun, including the straight wooden stock, curved magazine, and the trigger guard beneath the receiver.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the back of the wooden stock and the symmetry of the receiver and barrel, with the magazine barely visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the same features as view 1, with the magazine, trigger, and stock clearly visible from the other side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, medium length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
